title: Clarkia bottae
taxonomy: Clarkia bottae is a species of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae. It is a member of the genus Clarkia, which includes various species of flowers native to western North America. The plant is further classified into the tribe Onagreae, within the order Myrtales. The classification of Clarkia bottae is part of the larger taxonomic hierarchy of Onagraceae. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.

intro: Clarkia bottae, also known as Farewell-to-Kin or Bottle Clarkia, is a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family (Onagraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Clarkia bottae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Clarkia bottae include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote cardiovascular health and reduce stress.
